rationale: The cited paper appears as a single supporting reference in the results discussion, where the authors suggest Crestor's stronger detailing effect may be because it is the newest drug. It is used to provide background for interpreting one empirical pattern, not to develop the paper's core framework, hypotheses, or estimation approach. The citing study's main method and contribution (data enrichment with stated competitive reactions) are built on other sources, so this citation functions as a passing contextual mention.
